prompt: A high-concept, haunting editorial photograph. A hyper-realistic close-up of a human heart, looking visibly pale, sick, and withered. It is centered against a dark, textured background of deep, desaturated teal and shadowed black, providing maximum dramatic contrast. From each of its severed main tubes and vessels—the aorta, pulmonary artery, and venae cavae—vibrant, multi-colored synthetic plastic ribbons (shreds of blue shopping bags, yellow synthetic twine, and green packaging film) are flowing out, fluttering slightly like invasive detritus. NO text, NO labels, NO charts. Focused, cold, clinical chiaroscuro lighting emphasizes the textures of both the muscle and the plastic. 8k resolution, professional magazine cover aesthetic.
insight: Systemic microplastic contamination has moved beyond an environmental concern to a direct biological crisis, with synthetic industrial polymers now physically embedding themselves within critical human cardiovascular structures.
